channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Two syncope risk scores may help identify low-risk adults in the emergency department
item.description: The Canadian Syncope Risk Score and FAINT score effectively identify low-risk adult patients presenting to the ED with syncope.

item.title: Stronger patient-nurse bonds may improve mental health care and shorten stays
item.description: Being admitted to a mental health unit is often a vulnerable moment, but better relationships with nurses can transform care.

item.title: Online abuse affects one in four vulnerable youth, study finds
item.description: A new study from the Child Mind Institute finds negative online experiences are common among youth with mental health conditions, yet few report them.

item.title: Autoimmune disease linked to worse outcomes in myelodysplastic syndrome
item.description: A preexisting autoimmune disease is an independent risk factor for poor outcomes in myelodysplastic syndrome, according to a new study.
